The podium reflects the final Yahoo playoff result. The table also preserves each team’s regular-season record and playoff seed.
FinishTeamManagerRegular-season recordPlayoff seed

Awards & season moments

i
Awards recognize a season-long achievement. Moments capture a specific matchup that came down to one category, one late move or a razor-thin margin. Click a card for a 4:5 share preview.

Season awards first, then the close calls people will actually argue about in the group chat.

Swipe or scroll through the cards

Click any card to preview its future social-share format.

How the season evolved

i
Standings position uses cumulative matchup record through each week. A tie counts as half a win, and equal winning percentages share a rank. Category strength separately ranks that week’s nine category results, with turnovers reversed because fewer is better.

Standings by record

Weeks 1–20

Each cell shows the team’s league position and cumulative W–L–T record after that week. Teams with the same winning percentage share a position.

Weekly category strength

Regular season · Weeks 1–20

Complete data: 240 of 240 possible team-week results. Each cell shows that week’s rank and the average of the team’s nine category positions.

Postseason brackets

Weeks 21–23

Yahoo reseeded the championship bracket after the opening round. Green rows show each matchup winner.

Championship bracket

Consolation bracket

Placement games determine seventh through twelfth place.

The draft

i
The draft board shows every selection and final rank. Team draft value adds each pick’s positive BBM value above rank 157. It is a final-results review, not a judgment based on information available on draft night.
WorseBetter

Draft value by team

Sum above rank 157

Draft value vs. final finish

Original draft only

Draft leaderboard

RankTeamDraft value over rank 157Picks above rank 157Top contributorBest steal

Top drafted players

i
This list is ordered by final value above replacement. It identifies the most valuable players who were drafted, regardless of whether they were obvious early picks or late-round finds.
PlayerTeamPickFinal BBM rankValue above replacement

Best draft steals

i
Draft steals are ordered by how many places a player finished above his draft position. For example, pick 108 finishing 15th gains 93 places. Final value above replacement is included so the result still shows how useful the player actually was.
PlayerTeamDraftedFinal BBM rankPlaces gainedValue above replacement

Worst pick outcomes

i
Losses stop at replacement rank 157. Falling from useful to unrosterable matters; falling from rank 300 to 500 does not. Injuries count as outcomes, not proof that a pick was unreasonable.
PlayerTeamPickFinal BBM rankAdjusted rank lossContext

Roster management

i
These views compare waiver value, move volume, reliance on original draft picks, and how much each final roster changed from draft night.

Moves vs. value added by pickups

Labels show player actions

Value supplied by original draft picks

Higher means more draft-reliant

Final roster changed from draft night

Higher means more turnover

Best pickups

i
Subtract the BBM value of rank 157 from the player’s final value, then multiply by the fraction of the 167-day fantasy season that the team held him. Trades are excluded.
PlayerFantasy teamPicked upHeldEndFinal BBM rankValue × season heldContext

Best short-term pickups

i
These are waiver or free-agent pickups held 14 days or fewer and released before season end. Final value over rank 157 is multiplied by days held divided by 14. This does not measure the player’s exact statistics during those days.
PlayerFantasy teamPicked upHeldFinal BBM rankValue × days held ÷ 14

Manager styles

i
Horizontal position is the percentage of positive rostered value supplied by original draft picks. Vertical position is Yahoo’s move count. Circle size is completed trades. Color moves from cool to warm as the number of pickups held 14 days or fewer increases.
Left: less value from drafted playersRight: more value from drafted playersHigher: more movesLarger: more tradesWarmer: more pickups held ≤14 days

How the numbers are calculated

i
The report favors explicit calculations over composite mystery scores. These are the four formulas used most often across the page.
Weekly nine-category averageEach category is converted to a 0–100 position among that week’s teams. The nine positions are averaged equally.(FG% position + FT% position + … + turnover position) ÷ 9
Draft value over replacementFor every pick, subtract the BBM value of rank 157. Negative results become zero, then all 13 picks are added.Σ max(player BBM value − rank-157 value, 0)
Pickup value while heldA pickup’s value over replacement is multiplied by the fraction of the 167-day fantasy season that the team held him.value over replacement × days held in season ÷ 167
Trade value differenceFor each team in a trade, add the full-season value over replacement received and subtract the value sent.received value over replacement − sent value over replacement

Individual teams

i
Choose a team to see its complete draft, strongest pickup, weekly roster changes, injury-list placements and its 0–100 position in each scoring category.

Trades

i
Each trade appears once. For each team, the table adds the full-season BBM value over rank 157 received, then subtracts the same value sent. It does not yet isolate statistics produced after the trade date.

Who traded with whom

Thicker line = more completed trades between those managers
